title: [cross-file] standalone mode doesn't call lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort
description:
Interaction: main.c:init_standalone_lora <-> lora_l2.c:lichen_lora_l2_deinit

Bug: In main.c (CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 && !CONFIG_LICHEN_L2), the cleanup path at lines 127-128 calls:
  lichen_lora_l2_stop();
  lichen_lora_l2_deinit();

In lora_l2.c, lichen_lora_l2_deinit() at lines 872-874 has:
  #if defined(CONFIG_LICHEN_L2)
    lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort();
  #endif

When CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 is not defined (standalone mode), lichen_l2_reinit_after_abort() is not called. This is correct because rx_mutex doesn't exist without CONFIG_LICHEN_L2.

But the conditional is slightly wrong: it checks CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 at deinit time, but the rx_mutex exists whenever lichen_l2.c is compiled (which requires CONFIG_LICHEN_L2).

This is actually correct behavior - just checking that the cross-file interaction is intentional.

Actually this is NOT a bug. The guard is correct. When CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 is not defined, lichen_l2.c is not compiled, so there's no rx_mutex to reinit. The lora_l2.c code correctly guards the call.

Closing this as not a bug.
close_reason: Not a bug. When CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 is not defined, lichen_l2.c is not compiled, so rx_mutex does not exist. The guard at lora_l2.c:872 correctly skips the reinit call in standalone mode.
